models.application_remarks: Default ML model for optical microscopy hydride segmentation. Used as the repo-native trained checkpoint for GUI and CLI inference.
models.short_description: Default trained hydride checkpoint. Recommended before falling back to the conventional baseline.
models.detailed_description: Binary UNet checkpoint for hydride segmentation. The GUI now prefers this trained model at startup when the checkpoint is present locally; conventional inference remains available as an interactive fallback.

models.application_remarks: Compatibility alias for historical GUI records and CLI references that still use hydride_ml_Unet.
models.short_description: Legacy alias pointing to the default trained hydride checkpoint.
models.detailed_description: Retained for backward compatibility with older exported runs, docs, and scripts. Functionally resolves to the same candidate UNet checkpoint as hydride_ml.

models.application_remarks: Fast CPU baseline for hydride-like contrast patterns and quick QA workflows.
models.short_description: No checkpoint required; deterministic and easy to inspect.
models.detailed_description: Uses CLAHE, adaptive thresholding, and morphology. Best for baseline sanity checks and environments without GPU/ML dependencies.

models.application_remarks: Random-weight tiny smoke model for loading/inference/evaluation pipeline debug only.
models.short_description: Use only for CI/local smoke tests; not for scientific segmentation quality.
models.detailed_description: Generated by scripts/generate_smoke_checkpoint.py. Ensures model I/O orchestration works on clean machines without requiring large checkpoints.
